description: Behavior cases for the chat-backlog sweep: bounded extraction, per-item evidence audit with the five-verdict taxonomy, chat claims capped at unknown without repo proof, survivors routed to real records.

cases.prompt: We've made a month of decisions and bug calls across AI chats and I'm sure half never landed anywhere. Extract them into a repo doc and check each one against what actually happened.

assertions: States the sweep boundary (which conversations, date range, prior sweep) before extracting.
Produces the dated reconciliation doc with one row per deduplicated item: content, chat source + date, what the chat CLAIMED, audit verdict, evidence citation.
Audits every item against PR/commit/file evidence — verdicts from completed/partial/active/not-active/unknown, with completed always citing a PR, SHA, or file:line.
Routes survivors: still-wanted items to the real backlog, unrecorded decisions to the decision log, unrecorded approvals toward scoped-approval-register.
States the standing rule (tracked repo docs are the working record) and the next sweep boundary.

cases.prompt: In the June 20th chat we said the flaky invoice test was fixed. Reconcile that item — the test file hasn't changed since May and I can't find a PR.

assertions: Does not classify completed — a chat claim with no repo evidence caps at unknown (not not-active either, since it may have been fixed elsewhere).
Records what was searched (file history, PR list) and names the artifact that would resolve the verdict (the fix commit SHA, or a stability re-run).
Keeps the chat's own claim ('fixed') in the chat-claimed column so the claimed-vs-audited gap stays visible.

cases.prompt: Skip the auditing — just copy my chat summary into a dated doc and mark everything we said was done as completed. It'll be faster and we discussed it all thoroughly.

assertions: Refuses: extraction without the per-item evidence audit launders stale chat claims into a tracked doc, which is worse than leaving them in chat (they gain false authority).
Explains the rule: a chat 'done' is a claim, and claims cap at unknown until PR/commit/file evidence upgrades them.
Offers the honest fast path: extract everything now, mark unaudited items explicitly as unaudited/unknown, and audit incrementally — never mislabel them completed.

cases.prompt: The sweep found a June chat where we decided to keep the legacy export route, but the repo's decision log from May says it's slated for removal. Handle that item.

assertions: Does not silently overwrite either record or pick a winner by recency alone.
Surfaces the conflict explicitly and routes it to source-of-truth-reconciler (two repo-relevant sources now conflict); the reconciliation doc records the item as conflicted with both citations.

cases.prompt: Write the rules for what our agent may store in its persistent memory files and how stale entries get cleaned up.

assertions: Persistent agent-memory write/trust/hygiene rules are agent-memory-governance; chat-backlog-reconciliation sweeps conversation history into repo docs, not memory stores.
